items.name: XState
items.summary: A statechart library for authoring, executing, and visualizing complex app behavior.
items.why_included: HN treated it as the practical implementation behind the statecharts discussion.
items.evidence: The creator described XState as a JS/TS library, and commenters highlighted executable behavior, hierarchy, and visualization.

items.name: Dillo Browser
items.summary: A lightweight browser release aimed at staying usable on a less JavaScript-heavy web.
items.why_included: HN liked it as a small-browser counterpoint to modern web bloat.
items.evidence: Comments praised Dillo while debating Google’s JS requirements and suggesting proxy search or HTML-only fallbacks.

items.name: Asahi Linux
items.summary: An Asahi Linux progress report covering Apple Silicon audio and M3 support.
items.why_included: The thread focused on concrete platform advances for Linux on Apple hardware.
items.evidence: HN comments zeroed in on sample-rate support, M3 compatibility, and whether upstreaming or separation made more sense.

items.name: GnuPG
items.source_story_title: GnuPG – post-quantum crypto landing in mainline
items.why_included: A noteworthy crypto release with immediate migration implications.
items.evidence: The discussion centered on the new PQC algorithm, Windows improvements, and the 2.4 line reaching end-of-life.

items.name: Mine
items.source_story_title: Mine, a Coalton and Common Lisp IDE
items.summary: An old-school Common Lisp and Coalton IDE built for approachable editing.
items.why_included: Readers reacted to its QBASIC/Turbo-style friendliness.
items.evidence: Commenters said it sounded awesome and compared it to beginner-friendly Borland Turbo Pascal and QuickBasic environments.

items.name: VolSplat
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Turning a Gaussian Splat into a videogame
items.summary: A voxelize-and-mesh workflow for turning Gaussian splats into usable geometry.
items.why_included: Commenters pointed to it as a promising bridge from splats to editable 3D pipelines.
items.evidence: The thread linked VolSplat while discussing taking splats, making voxels, and meshing them for BIM-style workflows.

items.name: Clay PCB Tutorial
items.summary: A clay-circuit tutorial blending hardware craft, art, and ethical electronics.
items.why_included: The discussion was unusually lively around the materials and feminist framing.
items.evidence: Commenters called it a stonepunk art project, debated firing and energy use, and argued over what the feminist label means.

items.name: The Visible Zorker
items.summary: An interactive Zork 1 visualizer that exposes Z-machine internals.
items.why_included: HN commenters liked it as a neat way to inspect classic interactive fiction.
items.evidence: People said it showed Z-machine internals without a debugger and shared Infocom nostalgia around the demo.

items.name: Free textbook on engineering thermodynamics
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Free textbook on engineering thermodynamics
items.summary: A free engineering thermodynamics textbook with a downloadable PDF.
items.why_included: Readers engaged with the book itself and its self-publishing economics.
items.evidence: The author joined the thread, and commenters discussed the 40 MB PDF, Lulu costs, and whether the book’s price point made sense.

items.name: USB Cheat Sheet
items.summary: A USB reference cheat sheet that untangles version names and Thunderbolt overlap.
items.why_included: HN turned it into a useful practical guide on modern USB naming.
items.evidence: The thread spent most of its time correcting USB 3.x, USB4, and Thunderbolt terminology and clarifying real-world port capabilities.
